questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: A colorless water-soluble compound on strong heating liberates a brown colored gas and leaves a yellow residue that turns white on cooling. An aqueous solution of the original solid gives a white precipitate with $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{~S}$. The original solid is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Passing $\mathrm{H}_2\mathrm{~S}$ gas into a mixture of $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}, \mathrm{Ni}^{2+}, \mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Hg}^{2+}$ in an acidified aqueous solution precipitates

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: In qualitative inorganic analysis of a water-soluble salt mixture (salt $\mathrm{AB}+$ salt XY ) both the cations were identified as sulphides. In the tests for anions sodium carbonate extract when treated with $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$ gave yellowish precipitate soluble with difficulty in $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ while the other anion can be confirmed with brown ring test. (Given $\mathrm{K}_{\text {sp }}$ for $\mathrm{AS}=1 \times 10^{-44}$ and $\mathrm{XS}=1.4 \times 10^{-24}$ ). Identify the INCORRECT statement about the analysis.
options: $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ can be used under appropriate conditions of pH to separate and identify the cations.
Cation A will be precipitated under acidic condition as the concentration of sulphides ions required is low.
Cation X will be precipitated as sulphides under alkaline condition, as the concentration of sulphides ions required is very high.

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Addition of dil. HCl to an aqueous solution of a mixture of two inorganic salts yielded white precipitate $\mathbf{E}$ and filtrate $\mathbf{F}$. Precipitate $\mathbf{E}$ dissolved in hot water. $\mathbf{F}$ in alkaline alizarin gives a positive red lake test. The cations present in the precipitate $\mathbf{E}$ and solution $\mathbf{F}$ respectively are
